have h₁ : (u : 𝓞 K) ≠ 0 := by
    intro h
    have h₂ : (u : 𝓞 K) = 0 := h
    have h₃ : (u : (𝓞 K)ˣ) = 1 := by
      simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
    simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
  have h₂ : (v : 𝓞 K) ≠ 0 := by
    intro h
    have h₃ : (v : 𝓞 K) = 0 := h
    have h₄ : (v : (𝓞 K)ˣ) = 1 := by
      simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
    simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
  -- Simplify the expression by converting to K and using field operations
  have h₃ : ((u / v : 𝓞 K) : K) = (u : K) / (v : K) := by
    field_simp [h₂, Units.ext_iff]
    <;> norm_cast
    <;> simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
    <;> field_simp [h₂, Units.ext_iff]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> norm_cast
    <;> simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
  -- Use the simplified expression to prove the equality in K
  have h₄ : ((u / v : 𝓞 K) + 1 : K) = (u : K) / (v : K) + 1 := by
    rw [h₃]
    <;> simp [div_eq_mul_inv]
  have h₅ : ((u : K) / ((v : 𝓞 K) : K) + u / u : K) = (u : K) / (v : K) + 1 := by
    have h₅₁ : ((v : 𝓞 K) : K) = (v : K) := by norm_cast
    rw [h₅₁]
    have h₅₂ : (u : K) / (u : K) = 1 := by
      field_simp [h₁]
      <;> norm_cast
      <;> simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
    have h₅₃ : (u : K) / (v : K) + (u : K) / (u : K) = (u : K) / (v : K) + 1 := by
      rw [h₅₂]
    simpa [div_eq_mul_inv] using h₅₃
  -- Combine the equalities to prove the original statement
  have h₆ : ((u / v : 𝓞 K) + 1 : K) = ((u : K) / ((v : 𝓞 K) : K) + u / u : K) := by
    rw [h₄, h₅]
  -- Since the coercions are injective, we can conclude the proof in 𝓞 K
  norm_cast at h₆ ⊢
  <;> simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
  <;> field_simp [h₁, h₂] at *
  <;> ring_nf at *
  <;> norm_cast at *
  <;> simp_all [Units.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop
